title: Mallard Configuration
description: Budget, alert rules, and metric export configuration for the Mallard VS Code extension.

JsonOperand.description: A value or context field reference used as a comparison operand.

var.description: Dot-separated path into the rule context. Examples: "today.credits", "budget.percentOfBudget", "velocity.creditsPerHour", "vars.myThreshold"

JsonCondition.description: A boolean condition tree. Evaluated at runtime against the live usage snapshot.

oneOf.description: Literal true (always fires) or false (never fires).

and.description: All sub-conditions must be true (short-circuit AND).

or.description: At least one sub-condition must be true (short-circuit OR).

var.description: Truthy check on a context field. Example: {"var": "group.g1"} — fires when group g1 is active.

monthlyBudget.description: Monthly budget in USD. Set to 0 to disable budget tracking.

includedCredits.description: Premium requests included in the Copilot plan (used to colour the spend gauge).

dailyCreditAlert.description: Warn once per day when daily credit usage exceeds this threshold. Set to 0 to disable.

alerts.description: Built-in alert thresholds (independent of the custom rules system).

velocityCreditsPerHour.description: Alert when spending rate exceeds this credits/hour.

vars.description: User-defined variables. Reference in conditions as {"var": "vars.myThreshold"}.

groups.description: Named groups of rules. Set a group inactive to suppress all rules in it.

id.description: Unique group identifier. Referenced in rule active conditions as {"var": "group.<id>"}.

active.description: Condition that must be true for this group's rules to be evaluated.

rules.description: Custom alert and restriction rules evaluated against the live usage snapshot.

id.description: Unique rule identifier. Used for cooldown bookkeeping.

cooldown.description: Minimum time between firings. Examples: "1h", "30m", "1d". Defaults to 1h.

message.description: Alert message. Use {{ today.credits }} to insert live values via dot-path.

when.description: JSONLogic condition tree. Use 'conditions' for a simpler syntax.

conditions.description: Structured condition shorthand — easier to read and write than 'when'. Provide either 'when' or 'conditions', not both.

field.description: Dot-path into the rule context. Examples: "today.credits", "budget.percentOfBudget", "velocity.creditsPerHour".

op.description: Comparison operator. 'in': field value is one of an array. 'matches': field matches a regex/glob.

value.description: Value to compare against. For 'in', provide an array.

match.description: How to combine 'conditions'. 'all' = AND (default), 'any' = OR, 'none' = NOR. Ignored when 'when' is used.

active.description: Optional gate — rule is only evaluated when this is true. Omit to always evaluate.

requiresAuth.description: Skip this rule unless the user is signed in to GitHub.

notify.description: Whether to surface a VS Code notification when the rule fires.

thresholds.description: Severity escalation: instead of a single condition, declare multiple threshold levels. The highest-severity level whose condition fires wins.

severity.description: Severity to fire when this threshold is met.

cooldown.description: Per-level cooldown. Overrides the rule-level cooldown for this severity.

snoozeUntil.description: ISO 8601 timestamp. The rule is suppressed until this time. Typically managed via the dashboard UI.

restrict.description: Shows a restriction popup (Dismiss, Snooze, Disable Mallard) when this rule fires. Never disables any extension automatically.

reEnableWhen.description: Condition that, when true, automatically clears this restriction.

branchBudgets.description: Per-branch credit caps keyed by branch name. Reference as {"var": "currentBranchCredits"}.

githubBilling.description: GitHub billing authentication and scope. All fields are machine-scoped and never synced.

mode.description: Auth mode. 'vscode-session' uses VS Code's built-in GitHub OAuth. 'pat' uses the provided personal access token.

pat.description: Personal access token. Required when mode is 'pat'. Scopes: read:user for user billing, read:org for org billing.

org.description: GitHub organization slug. When set, fetches org-level billing instead of individual user billing. Requires read:org scope.

dashboard.description: Config-driven dashboard layout. Uses CSS grid shorthand syntax so values can be applied directly to grid-column and grid-row CSS properties. Config takes precedence over the layout saved in globalState.

columns.description: Number of columns in the dashboard grid.

panels.description: Panel declarations in display order. Panels not listed here fall back to the globalState layout.

gridColumn.description: CSS grid-column shorthand. 'span 2' makes the panel full width in a 2-column grid.

gridRow.description: CSS grid-row shorthand. Optional — defaults to 'span 1'.

hidden.description: Hide this panel. The panel can still be shown from the dashboard's edit mode.

size.description: Height tier for this panel's chart body. Defaults to 'normal'.

dailyBarsWindow.description: Number of days shown in the daily-bars and cumulative-area charts.

heatmapWeeks.description: Number of weeks shown in the activity heatmap.

topN.description: How many models and repos to show in ranked lists.
